chapter_title: About the Forbiddance of Being Undutiful to Parents

hadith_entries.text:
182. Narrated Abu-Bakra : 'The Prophet said thrice:
"Should I tell you about the most dangerous of the major
sins?" They said: "Yes, O Allah's Messenger!" He said: "To
join others in worship with Allah and to be undutiful to one's
:was reclining, then sat erect and said ﷺ parents." The Prophet
"Particularly, avoid perjury." Abu-Bakra said: 'The
Messenger of Allah kept on repeating this many times until
we said (to ourselves): "Would it be that he would keep
silent."""
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
أمَامَةَ

183. Narrated Abdullah ibn Onays Al-Johani: 'The
Messenger of Allah said: "It is of the major sins that one.
ascribes partners to Allah, is undutiful to parents, and
utters false oaths (that dips its taker in Fire)..."
-

184. Narrated Abdullah ibn Amr,may Allah be pleased
with her: Allah's Messenger said: "It is one of the
greatest sins that a man should curse his parents." It was
asked: "O Allah's Messenger! How does a man curse his
said: "The man abuses the father ﷺ parents?" The Prophet
of another man then, the latter abuses the father of the
former and abuses his mother."
Sho'aba : 'The

185. Narrated Al-Mogheera ibn Sho'aba
Prophet said: "Allah has forbidden for you to be
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
undutiful to your mothers, to bury your daughters alive, to
withhold the rights of the poor and others, and to beg from
men. And Allah has hated for you to gossip (sinful and vain
talk like backbiting etc.), to ask too many questions, (in
disputed religious issues, etc.), and He forbade you to
waste wealth (by extravagance with lack of wisdom and
thinking etc.)." (Bukhari)
*
